verses.text: If a soul hear swearing as witness but tells not — guilty. If touch unclean (beast, cattle, creeping thing, human) — when known, guilty. If swear to do good or evil — when known, guilty. He shall confess. Bring a female lamb or kid for sin offering. Priest atones. Guidance: God names ordinary sins — kept silent when truth was needed, careless touch, rash oaths. None are trivial. The first step is confession, the second a lamb.

The first step is confession, the second a lamb.

If he is not able to bring a lamb — bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons: one for sin, one for burnt offering. Priest sprinkles blood on the altar; drains the rest at the base. Offers the second as a burnt offering. Priest atones; he is forgiven. Guidance: For the poor, two doves. Same sin, same forgiveness, smaller cost. God's grace bends downward — toward whoever cannot afford the lamb. The atonement is real either way.

If he is unable to bring two doves — bring a tenth of an ephah of fine flour for sin offering. No oil, no frankincense. Priest takes a handful as memorial, burns on the altar. Priest atones; he is forgiven. Remnant is his. Guidance: For the poorest — flour, the same as bread. No oil, no incense, the celebration trimmings are off. But the grace is whole. God's altar receives the smallest gift as if it were the largest.

If a soul trespass through ignorance in the holy things — bring a ram without blemish, valued by silver shekels. Make amends, add a fifth, give to the priest. Priest atones; forgiven. If a soul sin against the commandments though he knew not — bring a ram, priest atones. Guidance: A trespass offering — for borrowing from the holy unintentionally. Restitution plus twenty percent, then a ram. God's mercy is real but not cheap: He restores what was wronged before He absolves.
